Understand first - 

1. CMP means current market price 

2. The range of buying is in between 0.016 to current market price 

3. Don’t be dumb to invest everything in one coin !! Invest according to your balance !! 

4. If you do leverage then don’t use too much that is going to cost your balance blowing up !! 

5. In case of ( SPOT ) written signals there won’t be any stoploss !! You just have to hold them and set the target point !! That’s why invest wisely and choose leverage wisely !! 

6. In cases of Target ~ you can do many things !! If you are a good holder you can wait till 3rd Target but if you are having less balance with you ~ you can simply sell everything in first target cause our first target also gains around 30% !! 

7. If you don’t find them in spot then you can simply find the coins on Alpha platform!! So instead of doing future all the time you can just buy them on Alpha 

8. The last and most important one ~ Don’t think of me as a person who randomly drops calls every single day without understanding any sorts of market sentiment!! I am very strict about it !! I will drop calls when I find it perfect for my group !! So don’t dm me regarding “ When will I drop next call?”


Trade with patient and don’t be greedy

Hello everyone,

I know many of you are feeling anxious after watching the recent market movements.

In some situations, there’s nothing we can do except let the market decide its direction. Regarding the calls — this month hasn’t been in our favor compared to the last three months, which were massive for us. We will not be giving any calls until we see a proper, high-probability setup.

Your subscription will not expire on the due date. We will extend it ourselves, so you don’t need to worry about that. You paid for quality calls, and until we deliver them properly, your subscription will not end.

Sometimes I do feel pressure because of the DMs asking why there are no calls. But I am responsible for 186 premium members — not just one or two. If calls don’t work properly, ultimately the responsibility falls on me, even though it would be completely unintentional.

I genuinely care about protecting your assets first. So please be patient regarding calls and don’t pressure me to drop trades. It will not be beneficial for you

Stay calm. War situations are often opportunities for accumulation.

We’ve already lived through worst-case scenarios: 
• Oct 10 crash
• Tariff wars
• Epstein file panic
Markets have absorbed massive shocks already.

Right now, this feels like a bear trap

If USA strikes, it likely won’t drag long.
And Iran isn’t a country that can sustain a prolonged direct conflict with the US.

But here’s the key macro angle 
A long war doesn’t benefit the US either.

Why? 
• Massive debt pressure 39T
• Fragile macro environment
• Liquidity already tight

In both scenarios, prolonged escalation hurts everyone  especially the US economy.

Zoom out.
Think macro
6
2
249

19:06

Bitcoin is down only 2–3% even with full war headlines.
But small bullish catalysts are triggering upside reactions.

That’s interesting.
When a market:
• Ignores bad news
• Moves on small good news
It usually means sellers are getting exhausted.
We’ve already had crashes, macro fear, and liquidity flushes  yet downside is weakening.
This is classic reaction asymmetry.
Markets don’t bottom when news turns good.
They bottom when bad news stops working.

Not calling the bottom, but the market is starting to behave like one that’s running out of sellers.
Stay sharp. The shift comes when least expected.

Some people are trading futures without stop loss and using high leverage on coins like $TNSR 

At the same time, they’re putting big money into meme coins like $MUBARAK 

I’ve said this before  use small amounts for meme coins.
They are HIGH risk.

You are not gambling.
You are trading.

Loss is part of trading.
But if you treat trading like gambling, you won’t grow.

Yes, you might make profit sometimes.
But one single high-leverage trade without a stop loss can liquidate your entire capital.

Real growth doesn’t come from lucky trades.
It comes from proper risk management.

Survive first.
Then grow.

“Bull market” is dead.

And most of you are still waiting for it like idiots.
Meanwhile, whales are printing money.

Wake up.
Retail is broke.
No liquidity = no real bull run.

So, what did smart money do?
They changed the game.

New model:

Pump → Hype → Trap → Dump

10x, 20x, 50x… then destroy late buyers.

And guess who provides exit liquidity? It's you.

This isn’t investing anymore.
This is a hunting game.

 Either you adapt

Or you get farmed

Smart traders: 
• Enter early
• Take profits fast
• No emotions
You don’t fight whales.
You ride them — or get wrecked.

The market is heavily influenced by whales and institutions — not just in crypto, but even in traditional markets through insider activity.

Right now, liquidity is low and real buying volume is weak. This makes it easier for big players to move price and hunt retail traders’ stop losses.


 If you’re not experienced with stop-loss hunting and market manipulation tactics, be careful.


My role is simple:
I provide high-probability trade setups.


Your job:
Manage risk wisely and avoid getting wiped out by obvious liquidity traps.


 Blindly placing tight SL in these conditions can get you liquidated fast.


Trade smart. Stay patient. Follow structure — not emotions

The last 4 calls — $TA, $FHE, $TAKE, $PIXEL — were all in profit. However, we observed a recurring pattern: sudden 15-minute bearish candles triggering stop losses, followed by either a sharp bounce or continuation.
This is not random behavior. It reflects current market conditions where liquidity is being aggressively targeted. High-frequency volatility and stop hunts are dominating lower timeframes, making even A+ setups vulnerable to premature stop-outs.

If your SL is getting hit in this market, don’t panic.

Here’s a simple strategy:
Use EMA 21 & 50 for entries, EMA 200 as key support, and confirm with RSI for trend direction.

This is not random—institutions also use these levels to build and execute their positions.

Right now, the market is driven by whales & institutions—not retailers. So instead of fighting them, align with them.

No strategy is 100%—losses are part of trading. Stay disciplined, manage risk, and focus on consistency.

Trading isn’t gambling — we deliver high-accuracy calls with one priority: protect your capital first, then grow it.

You don’t need 20 trades a week.
2–3 solid wins are enough.
Overtrading is the fastest way to lose everything.

If you want real growth, you need patience.
If you want to gamble — this isn’t your place.

Success in trading comes down to 3 things:

1. Discipline


2. Patience (wait for confirmation)


3. Booking profits & accepting losses



No FOMO entries. No emotional trades.

I’m not here to scam — this is a business.
This channel runs like a modern-day brokerage with one goal: make you consistently profitable.

I know some of my HIGH TABLE members can't make money even in this setup.

So this advice for them to listen carefully

Most traders don’t fail because of strategy — they fail because of money management.

Simple example:
If you have $1000, don’t go big.
Take small trades like $10 per trade.Use 5x leverage.

Always use Stop Loss and Take Profit.
No greed, no overtrading.
Just follow this for 1 month.

You will learn: 
• how to control emotions
• how to manage risk
• how to stay consistent

Important truth: If you can manage $10 properly, you can manage $1000 or $10,000 properly too.

Start small. Learn the process. Stay disciplined.
Trading is a profession, not gambling

Trading every day isn’t real trading — it’s overtrading.

From a mathematical and strategic point of view, it doesn’t make sense. Markets need breathing space, and so do you. Even money flow isn’t constant — it cycles.

The market typically provides cleaner opportunities on

• Monday

• Tuesday

• Wednesday

The rest of the time? Often noise, manipulation, or low-probability setups.

If you call yourself a trader, then understand this:
It’s not about being in the market every day — it’s about understanding how money actually flows.

You’re here for financial freedom, not for constant action.

Protect your capital first.
Because if you can protect your money, you’re already close to becoming a successful trader.

You take an A+ setup…
Still hit SL 2–3 times in a row.

Confidence drops.
You start thinking… “Is crypto even for me?”

Relax. This phase hits every trader.

Last week?
Everything = TP 

This week?
Everything = SL 

Why?

Because the market isn’t normal right now.

 Low liquidity
 FOMC pressure
 Big players waiting on new Fed direction

That means:
Random pumps ≠ real trends
Whales are playing, not the market.

So don’t chase. Don’t overtrade.
Wait for liquidity to return.

The same market that gives SL today
Will give 100%+ tomorrow.

 Loss is part of the game
 Discipline is what keeps you alive

We wait. We strike when it’s time.

MARKET UPDATE FOR THE "HIGH TABLE"

Right now we have many active calls running. Some already hit TP, while others are still moving toward their direction. This is a normal part of the journey.

The market is currently in a mid-liquidity phase, and narratives are changing very fast. In this type of environment, patience and risk management matter more than chasing every move.

My suggestion: 
 Secure around 10% spot profit whenever possible
 Protect your capital first
 Don’t expect every trade to become a moonshot

Remember — we do NOT need to win every trade.

Out of 10 trades, even 3-5 solid winners are enough to stay profitable and grow consistently. That’s how smart traders survive long term.

We are still in a bear market structure, and during times like this, survival is more important than ego.

Protect capital. Stay disciplined. Stay patient.
The people who survive this phase are the ones who will dominate the next bull run. 

If you are stuck in any trades and need any suggestions, please  DM our analyst @tucosalamanca98
